steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence says Stephaney Smith worked at JPL, one of the world's leading facilities for robotic space exploration. The second sentence gives a concrete detail of what she did there — her specific role on the Mars 2020 Perseverance rover team.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence narrows in on a concrete example of Smith's work at JPL.
  • Choice A — Conversely,: signals direct opposition, but Smith's rover role doesn't contradict the claim that she worked at JPL — it confirms it. ✗
  • Choice B — Finally,: signals the last item in a series or the end of a process, but there's no series here — just a general claim followed by one detail. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: signals contrast despite an obstacle, but no obstacle is mentioned in the first sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Specifically,: signals a concrete detail or narrowing of a general claim, so fits — the rover role is the specific work she did at JPL. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
